channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This messaging mecca integrates Slack, WhatsApp, and Telegram
item.description: I like chatting with my friends—who doesn’t?—but I don’t always know where to find them. There are simply too many apps. Some of my friends text, others use WhatsApp, while others yet insist on using Discord or the DM feature in whatever random social network they prefer.
item.dc:creator: Justin Pot, Fast Company

item.title: Cybercriminals targeted a gaming hosting provider with one of the biggest DDoS attacks ever - 6TBps assault ranks in the top 10 of the largest DDoS onslaughts recorded
item.description: Gcore’s network withstood a record DDoS assault, highlighting escalating short-burst attacks and growing threats to gaming infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Will challenger banks start to launch their own mobile networks to keep their customers happy? This may well be a defining trend in 2026
item.description: Neobanks are venturing into telecom through MVNO models, using eSIM technology to link mobile connectivity with digital financial ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: What Sora’s Martin Luther King Jr. problem revealed to OpenAI
item.description: Martin Luther King Jr. did plenty to change the world for the better. And nearly 60 years after his assassination, he’s at the center of a major concession by the world’s leading AI company that puts a battle over intellectual property and the right to control one’s image into the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Claude just got a skill upgrade and it’s coming for your workflows
item.description: Model builders are moving beyond simple AI chatbots to creating comprehensive assistants that, in the words of AI dignitary Ethan Mollick, “do real work” in enterprise workflows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House joins Bluesky and immediately trolls Trump opponents
item.description: The White House has joined Bluesky, the social media platform of choice of many in the left-leaning online world.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Toonstar’s new ‘Uncle Roger’ cartoon embraces AI—but slop it’s not
item.description: “The gyoza needs to look a little whiter. It’s too pink.” Nigel Ng is genially micromanaging the look and feel of Fried, an animated series that will premiere on YouTube later this year.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Shaping enterprise talent strategies in the age of AI
item.description: AI, and in particular agentic AI, present an opportunity to rethink the nature of work, reshaping teams and blurring the line between technology and talent leadership.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Putting AI agents to work in IT
item.description: Over the next 12 to 24 months, AI agents are expected to revolutionize every facet of business. According to PwC’s AI Agent Survey, 75% of respondents believe AI agents will reshape the workplace even more than the Internet did in the past.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: I helped build the internet’s ad economy. Now I want to save it
item.description: If you’ve noticed that the internet feels different lately—more cluttered, harder to navigate—you’re not imagining it. The system is breaking down in real time, and by 2026, researchers predict that 90% of web content will be AI-generated.
item.dc:creator: Brian O'Kelley, Fast Company

item.title: Here are the winners of Instagram’s inaugural award for groundbreaking creators
item.description: A makeup illusionist, a photography project, and an innovative DJ are among the winners of Instagram’s inaugural Rings awards.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: AI-powered Bay Area home insurance startup expands in risky markets
item.description: San Francisco-based Stand uses remote sensing data and collects details from homeowners. The startup analyzes this information using AI, which it uses to simulate how wind, heat, embers and other factors stand to contribute to possible damage.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta adds parental controls for AI-teen interactions
item.description: Meta is adding parental controls to kids’ interactions with artificial intelligence chatbots — including the ability to turn off one-on-one chats with AI characters altogether.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Hollow Knight: Silksong patch looks to improve its Simplified Chinese translation among other fixes
item.description: The latest Hollow Knight: Silksong patch brings overhauled Simplified Chinese translation following launch backlash.
item.dc:creator: Rhys Wood, TechRadar

item.title: AI pushes CIOs to redefine IT roles for the future
item.description: AI is already reshaping the IT job landscape, with layoffs and demand for AI skills creating a mismatched market for IT skills. But the technology is also contributing to possible contraction for IT workforces as enterprises IT leaders find themselves redefining IT roles in the wake of AI’s rise.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Agents of chaos
item.description: The dream of self-directed AI is becoming real, and companies can’t wait to turn their agents loose on everyday workflows. Standards like the Model Context Protocol are helping agents move from chat to action, while new frameworks promise smoother integrations and smarter automation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia sharing tips on curbing social media for children before age limit starts in December
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — The Australian government has begun a public education campaign with tips on how to wean children off social media ahead of a world-first national 16-year age limit taking effect in December.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta details cutting-edge networking technologies for AI infrastructure
item.description: Meta shared details about its AI and networking advances at this week’s 2025 OCP Global Summit in San Jose, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arm joins Open Compute Project to build next-generation AI data center silicon
item.description: Chip designer Arm Holdings plc has announced it is joining the Open Compute Project to help address rising energy demands from AI-oriented data centers. Arm said it plans to support companies in developing the next phase of purpose-built silicon and packaging for converged infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Booker courts red state voters online, urging them to join ‘No Kings’ protests
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) is pressing red state voters to join local “No Kings” protests Saturday and put pressure on their GOP legislators to end the government shutdown through his outreach with social media influencers and creators in Florida and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, The Hill

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta to invests $1.5 billion for El Paso AI data center
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) - Facebook's parent company, Meta, is going to be building a new artificial intelligence center in El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Jamel Valencia, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats press Google, YouTube on Trump settlement
item.description: Several Senate Democrats are pressing Google and YouTube for answers about their decision to settle a lawsuit with President Trump over YouTube’s suspension of his account in the wake of the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol. In a Wednesday letter to Google and YouTube executives, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft accused of trying to manipulate AI pricing via its OpenAI investment
item.description: Microsoft, which is no stranger to antitrust accusations, is being sued for allegedly manipulating AI prices via its investment in OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Group including Nvidia, BlackRock buying Aligned Data Centers in deal worth about $40 billion
item.description: A group including BlackRock, Nvidia and Microsoft is buying Aligned Data Centers in an approximately $40 billion deal in an effort to expand next-generation cloud and artificial intelligence infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Selective retraining helps AI learn new skills without forgetting, study finds
item.description: A new study from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign suggests that the loss of skills often seen when fine-tuning large AI models may not be true forgetting but a temporary bias in their output.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The best Java microframeworks to learn now
item.description: Java microframeworks are generally lightweight and minimalistic, characterized by their emphasis on performance, cloud-native capabilities, and developer experience. They are designed for building lightweight web applications, and they’re particularly well-suited to microservices architectures.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lawsuit accuses Amazon of 'fake' Prime Day deals
item.description: Two people are suing Amazon, accusing the retail giant of advertising "fake sales" and misleading consumers during the company's popular "Prime Day" events.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: US indicts 'mastermind' of Cambodian cyberscam network, seizes $15B in Bitcoin
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Tuesday it has indicted the alleged orchestrator of a Cambodia-based cryptocurrency fraud scheme. The indictment, filed Oct.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: Oracle targets agentic use cases with AI Database 26ai
item.description: Oracle is doubling down its focus on agentic use cases for enterprises with new features added to its latest long-term support version of its database offering — AI Database 26ai. The new database includes Select AI Agent, Agent Factory, and integration with Oracle’s MCP servers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SpaceX launches the 11th test flight of its mega Starship rocket with another win
item.description: SpaceX launched another of its mammoth Starship rockets on a test flight Monday, successfully making it halfway around the world while releasing mock satellites like last time. Starship — the biggest and most powerful rocket ever built — thundered into the evening sky from the southern tip of Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, Fox 5
